question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send contract call requests with a gas value exactly equal to or below GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,000) so that scaleGas() returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) to always succeed and bypass the gas throttle entirely, allowing unlimited EVM executions that increase node CPU/memory consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() floor-division precision loss by sending requests with gas values just above multiples of 10,000 (e.g., gas=10,001 consumes only 1 token but executes with 10,001 gas), achieving a near-2x amplification of EVM work per gas token consumed from the gasLimitBucket, increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on the rateLimitBucket (unlike gasLimitBucket which uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED) to cause race conditions under concurrent requests, allowing more requests than the configured requestsPerSecond limit to pass through and increase node processing load by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on the opcodeRateLimitBucket (default LOCK_FREE strategy) to bypass the 1-RPS limit on the heavy opcode endpoint under concurrent requests, triggering multiple expensive opcode trace executions simultaneously and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on the rateLimitBucket to burst the full requestsPerSecond capacity (500 requests) at the very start of each 1-second window, causing a spike in node CPU/memory usage that sustains over 30% higher resource consumption compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on the gasLimitBucket to burst the full gasPerSecond capacity at the start of each second window, submitting maximum-gas requests in a single burst that forces the node to execute the maximum possible EVM work per second, increasing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft contract call requests that always revert (e.g., calling a non-existent function), causing restoreGasToBucket() to restore 100% of the gasLimit back to the gasLimitBucket (since gasLimitRefundPercent defaults to 100), effectively recycling gas tokens indefinitely and allowing sustained high-gas EVM executions that increase node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send estimate=true requests (isEstimate flag) to trigger the binary search gas estimation path in ContractExecutionService.estimateGas(), which calls callContract() up to maxGasEstimateRetriesCount (20) times per single throttle check, amplifying EVM execution work by up to 20x per gas token consumed from the gasLimitBucket and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user combine the estimate=true flag with the maximum allowed gas limit (15,000,000) to pass a single rateLimitBucket token check but trigger up to 20 full EVM executions at maximum gas, causing a 20x amplification of CPU/memory usage per rate-limit token and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (a global lock) to cause lock contention by sending many concurrent requests, forcing all threads to serialize on the gasLimitBucket lock and increasing thread wait time and CPU overhead by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the global (non-per-IP, non-per-user) nature of the rateLimitBucket to consume the entire 500 RPS capacity from a single source, starving other users while simultaneously maximizing node processing load with computationally expensive contract calls, increasing overall node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the global (non-per-IP, non-per-user) nature of the gasLimitBucket to consume the entire gasPerSecond budget with a single carefully timed request carrying the maximum gas limit, preventing other users from executing while the node processes the maximum-gas EVM execution, increasing resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket's 1-RPS limit combined with the absence of SynchronizationStrategy to send two concurrent opcode requests that both pass tryConsume(1) before either decrements the token count, triggering two simultaneous heavy opcode trace executions and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sequence of requests where each request's gas value is just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas=10,001) to consume only 1 token per request from the gasLimitBucket while the EVM executes with the full 10,001 gas, achieving a sustained amplification ratio that increases node resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bandwidth.builder().refillGreedy() configuration on the rateLimitBucket to send exactly requestsPerSecond requests at the start of each second window, then immediately send another burst at the next second boundary, achieving a sustained maximum throughput that keeps node CPU utilization elevated by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call with estimate=true and a gas value just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (10,001) so that scaleGas() consumes only 1 token from the gasLimitBucket, but the binary search estimation triggers up to 20 EVM executions each using up to 10,001 gas, creating a 200,000x amplification of EVM work per gas token and increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the in-memory (non-distributed) nature of the rateLimitBucket to bypass rate limits when the deployment has multiple web3 service instances, since each instance maintains its own independent bucket with the full capacity, effectively multiplying the allowed RPS by the number of instances and increasing aggregate node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the in-memory (non-distributed) nature of the gasLimitBucket across multiple web3 service instances to submit high-gas requests to each instance simultaneously, with each instance independently allowing the full gasPerSecond budget, multiplying total EVM execution work and increasing aggregate node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gas restore mechanism (restore() calling gasLimitBucket.addTokens()) by sending requests that fail with gasUsed == gasLimit, causing the full gasLimit to be restored to the bucket, then immediately reusing those restored tokens for another high-gas request, creating a cycle that sustains maximum EVM execution load and increases node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-connection or per-session limits in the ThrottleConfiguration to open many persistent HTTP connections and pipeline requests, consuming the full rateLimitBucket capacity from a single client while maximizing server-side connection handling overhead, increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bandwidth.builder().capacity(gasLimit).refillGreedy(gasLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) configuration to observe that the bucket starts full, then time requests to arrive exactly when the bucket refills to maximum capacity, achieving maximum gas consumption per second and sustaining node resource consumption over 30% higher than the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket's refillGreedy strategy to send one opcode request per second continuously, where each opcode trace execution is described as 'heavy', sustaining maximum opcode processing load on the node and increasing resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests with deeply nested or complex contract call data payloads that pass the rateLimitBucket check (consuming only 1 token) but trigger expensive database queries and EVM state loading, exploiting the fact that the rateLimitBucket only counts requests without considering their computational weight, increasing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED while the rateLimitBucket does not, to craft a timing attack where many threads pass the unsynchronized rateLimitBucket check concurrently before any of them reaches the synchronized gasLimitBucket, causing a thundering herd on the gasLimitBucket lock and increasing node CPU overhead by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rateLimitBucket check (tryConsume(1)) occurs before the gasLimitBucket check, so that a request consuming 1 rate-limit token but carrying maximum gas (15,000,000) forces the node to perform gas scaling computation and gasLimitBucket contention even when the gas bucket is nearly empty, wasting CPU cycles and increasing resource consumption by over 30%?
